commands.review_summary:
The PR propagates TTS `spokenText` through pending tool-media block replies and expands the trusted local-media path from raw `tts` to owned `tts`/`openclaw.tts` tool results.

PR surface: Source +35, Tests +19. Total +54 across 11 files.

Reproducibility: yes. from source: current main filters a local `/tmp` media path for raw `openclaw.tts` when the exact trusted tool-name set omits it, so the media is not queued for channel delivery.

target.title: fix: deliver tts tool audio on whatsapp
target.body:
## Summary

This fixes `tts` tool audio delivery for channel-backed runs such as WhatsApp.

Before this change, a successful `tts` tool call only returned a local audio path in `details.media`. Delivery then depended on the generic tool-result media extraction path noticing that media and trusting the tool name. That path can miss qualified OpenClaw tool names such as `openclaw.tts`, so the transcript could show the spoken text while the generated audio never reached WhatsApp.

The fix sends successful TTS media through the normal `routeReply` channel delivery path when the agent run has channel delivery context. After that inline send succeeds, the tool result marks the same media as `outbound: false`, which keeps the transcript details available without letting generic tool-result media handling send the audio a second time.

## What changed

- Pass the current run's channel target, thread id, session key, account id, and run id into `createTtsTool`.
- Route successful TTS output as a `ReplyPayload` with `mediaUrl`, `spokenText`, `trustedLocalMedia`, and `audioAsVoice` when delivery context is present.
- Preserve fallback behavior for contexts without a delivery target: the tool still returns `details.media` for the generic media path.
- Treat both `tts` and `openclaw.tts` as OpenClaw-owned TTS tool names for trusted local media.
- Keep external or MCP-backed TTS-looking results untrusted even if they set `trustedLocalMedia`.

## Safety notes

- The inline send goes through `routeReply`, so channel behavior stays behind the existing message delivery boundary instead of adding a WhatsApp-specific send path inside the tool.
- `details.media.outbound = false` is only set after `routeReply` returns success; failed route delivery throws and does not silently hide the media.
- The local-media trust exception is limited to owned TTS tool names and still rejects external tool results.

## Real behavior proof

Behavior addressed: TTS tool audio generated during a WhatsApp/channel-backed agent run is delivered through channel routing instead of relying only on later tool-result media extraction.

Real environment tested: Local unit/integration-level Vitest shards in this worktree.

Exact steps or command run after this patch: `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/agents/tools/tts-tool.test.ts src/agents/embedded-agent-subscribe.tools.media.test.ts`

Evidence after fix: `tts-tool.test.ts` verifies `routeReply` receives the synthesized media payload with WhatsApp delivery context and that the returned media is marked `outbound: false`; `embedded-agent-subscribe.tools.media.test.ts` verifies `openclaw.tts` trusted local media is retained and `external.tts` is rejected.

Observed result after fix: 2 Vitest shards passed, 54 tests total.

What was not tested: Live WhatsApp Web delivery with a real linked account was not run for this PR.
target.author: mcaxtr
